mnemonics.title: STRING FUNCTIONS MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: STRING MEMORY TRICK: COPY (strcpy) goes DEST-SRC, CAT (strcat) adds to END, CMP compares LEXICO (dictionary order). Remember: DEST Before SOURCE always.

mnemonics.title: CTYPE FUNCTIONS MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: ALPHA DIGIT ALNUM SPACE: A function with 'is' returns TRUE/FALSE (non-zero/zero). Remember: IS-ALPHA tests letters, IS-DIGIT tests numbers, IS-ALNUM tests both.

mnemonics.title: MALLOC FAMILY MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: MALLOC vs CALLOC: M-alloc = ONE chunk, garbage values. C-alloc = CLEARED to zero, COUNT of chunks. Remember: CALLOC = CLEAN + COUNT.

mnemonics.title: MATH FUNCTIONS MEMORY AID
mnemonics.content: MATH FUNCTIONS: S-quare root (sqrt), P-ower (pow), ABS-olute (abs for int, fabs for float). Remember: S-P-A (Square root, Power, Absolute).

acronyms.usage: Header containing printf, scanf, gets, puts, getchar, putchar for I/O operations.
Header containing malloc, calloc, free, realloc, atoi, itoa, rand, srand, exit, abs.
Header containing isalpha, isdigit, isalnum, isspace, toupper, tolower for character classification.
Header containing strlen, strcpy, strcat, strcmp, strupr, strlwr, strrev for string operations.
Header containing sqrt, pow, sin, cos, tan, floor, ceil, fabs for mathematical calculations.

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a ruler measuring characters from start to the NULL terminator (\0). The ruler shows length but doesn't count the endpoint mark.
Think of malloc() as reserving a hotel room of specific size. calloc() reserves multiple rooms AND cleans them (sets to 0). free() checks out the room.
Think of dictionary ordering - words closer to 'A' are 'less than' words closer to 'Z'. Compare first differing character ASCII values.
srand() is like shuffling a deck of cards. rand() draws cards from shuffled deck. Same shuffle = same card sequence every time.

analogies.analogy: Like counting pages in a book from page 1 until you hit the last page - doesn't count the cover.
Like photocopying a document - creates an exact copy at a new location while original remains unchanged.
Like attaching a trailer to a truck - the trailer (source) gets connected to the back of the truck (destination).
Renting a storage unit - you request a specific size, system allocates space, you get a key (pointer) to access it.
Rolling a die - each roll gives unpredictable numbers, but patterns may repeat over many rolls (pseudo-random).
